note: Answer key for a candidate-selection task. Each template's shipped manifest (sources/template_candidates/<id>.json) declares its item-metadata schema fields and features. Offline + frozen -> deterministic. The conflict: the prettiest gallery/scrollytelling templates lack archival provenance fields, the full-metadata templates lack a timeline, and only one template has BOTH the complete provenance schema AND a timeline AND IIIF.
use_case: Small museum/archive online photo exhibition. Photos require provenance metadata; exhibition is chronological (timeline); images are high-res (IIIF).
hard_rule: a template is eligible only if its schema includes ALL four required fields AND its features include both timeline and IIIF large images

per_candidate.note: only template with full provenance schema (caption/credit/rights/accession) + timeline + IIIF

per_candidate.trap: NEAR-MISS: full Dublin-Core metadata incl rights+accession and IIIF, but the base exhibit theme has no built-in timeline (needs the separate Neatline plugin). Must be rejected unless that gap is resolved.
NEAR-MISS: full provenance schema + IIIF, but no timeline component.
BEAUTY TRAP: gorgeous scrollytelling timeline, but the schema has no rights and no accession_number, and no IIIF large images — provenance-incomplete.
BEAUTY TRAP: stunning lightbox gallery, but only title/caption/date/tags — no credit, rights, or accession; unusable for archival provenance.

gold.uniqueness: Only template satisfying all required provenance fields AND timeline AND IIIF. No tie-break needed.
gold.acceptable_alternative: omeka_classic_exhibit or wax_minimal_archive ONLY IF the executor explicitly identifies the missing timeline and proposes a concrete way to add it (e.g. Neatline for Omeka) -> up to ~0.85, because they have the full provenance schema

key_facts.photofolio_is_a_beauty_trap: must NOT be picked; missing credit/rights/accession
key_facts.exhibit_timeline_story_is_a_beauty_trap: has the timeline but missing rights/accession + IIIF
key_facts.omeka_wax_near_miss: full metadata but no timeline — only acceptable with an explicit fix

scoring_anchors.note: The discriminator is the archival provenance schema (rights + accession + credit), which the pretty gallery/scrollytelling templates lack, combined with the timeline requirement that the full-metadata templates lack. Only collectionbuilder_archive has all of it. Executors should cite each manifest's item_metadata_fields and features as evidence.
